nativeRange.habitat: Riverbanks and shaded stream margins in West African tropical rainforest; grows epiphytically attached to wood, roots, and rocks at the water margin, often emersed in nature

decorPreferences: shaded areas to reduce leaf algae

goodWith: African cichlids (tough leaves resist most nibbling)

summary: A cultivar of Anubias barteri prized for distinctive corrugated leaves that emerge bronze/coffee-colored before maturing to deep green. Hardy, slow-growing, beginner-friendly; rhizome must never be buried.
careNotes: Anubias barteri var. coffeefolia ('Coffee Leaf Anubias') is a popular cultivar of Anubias barteri, distinguished by its rippled, corrugated leaf surface and the striking coffee-bronze color of newly emerging leaves, which mature to a deep glossy green over time. Husbandry is essentially identical to the parent species (see fw-plant-004, 'Anubias'): it is a slow-growing epiphytic rhizome plant that thrives in low-to-medium light, tolerates a wide range of water parameters, and does not require CO2 (though injection accelerates growth). Like all Anubias, it has one CRITICAL care requirement: the rhizome (the thick horizontal stem from which leaves and roots emerge) MUST stay above substrate. Burying the rhizome causes it to rot and the plant to melt and die. Attach the plant to driftwood, rocks, or other hardscape using thread, fishing line, or cyanoacrylate (super) glue; alternatively, wedge it into a crevice. Roots will anchor on their own over time. Adult height is most commonly reported at 10–15 cm (4–6 in), with well-established specimens reaching up to ~25 cm (10 in) — making it a medium-sized cultivar, smaller than the broad-leaf parent (25–45 cm) but larger than 'Nana Petite'. Best suited to midground placement, where the distinctive ruffled leaves and new-growth coloration are most visible. Lighting in the low range is preferred and produces the best leaf coloration without encouraging algae; brighter lighting can speed leaf production but increases algae buildup on the slow-growing leaves. Placing the plant in shaded areas of the tank (under taller plants, behind hardscape) is the most reliable way to keep leaves algae-free. CO2 is optional but reported to improve leaf quality and growth rate. Liquid fertilization is beneficial; substrate quality is largely irrelevant since nutrient uptake is via water-column roots. Cross-source notes: temperature range 22–28 °C anchored to consensus with the parent species and Modern Aquarium's wide tolerance range (18–30 °C / 65–86 °F); pH range 6.0–7.5 from Buce Plant; gH 2–15 inherited from the parent-species cross-source consensus. Modern Aquarium does not state pH or hardness; Buce Plant does not state temperature.
breedingNotes: Propagated by rhizome division: with a sharp blade, cut a healthy rhizome into sections, ensuring each section retains at least 3–4 healthy leaves and some attached roots. Re-attach each section to hardscape — do not bury. As with all Anubias, occasional underwater flowering (small white spathe-and-spadix inflorescence typical of Araceae) is possible but seed propagation is not practical in the home aquarium.

primary.name: Modern Aquarium
primary.notes: Primary source page for Anubias barteri var. coffeefolia. Confirmed origin (West Africa), care level (easy), light demand (low to moderate), CO2 (not required), growth rate (slow), midground placement, epiphyte/rhizome handling, temperature tolerance 65–86 °F (18–30 °C), and rippled/corrugated leaf morphology.

additional.name: Buce Plant
additional.notes: Cross-checked family (Araceae), origin (West Africa), care level (easy), pH (6.0–7.5), light (low), CO2 (not necessary but accelerates growth), growth rate (slow to moderate), adult size (4–6 inches typical, up to ~12 inches / ~30 cm), midground-to-background placement, propagation (rhizome division), and the coffee-colored new-growth maturing to dark green. Buce Plant also explicitly warns that burying the rhizome causes melt and death.

additional.name: Anubias barteri (fw-plant-004) – AquaMate sister entry
additional.notes: Cultivar-of-parent reference. Coffeefolia is a cultivar of A. barteri and shares the same fundamental care profile (epiphytic habit, rhizome handling, lighting/CO2 demand, parameter tolerances). Used to confirm temperature and gH ranges where coffeefolia-specific sources were silent.
